description: Adding three CHECK constraints to a 90M-row subscriptions table on a high-traffic billing system. Review locking, validation, and ordering of operations.
review_checklist: Distinguish ALTER TABLE ADD CONSTRAINT (validating) from ADD CONSTRAINT NOT VALID + VALIDATE
Identify lock acquisition implications of synchronous validation
Verify CONCURRENTLY usage on partial indexes for production tables
Check whether existing data violates the proposed constraints

ground_truth.description: ALTER TABLE ADD CONSTRAINT CHECK without NOT VALID acquires AccessExclusiveLock and synchronously scans every row in subscriptions (90M rows, ~8 minutes). All reads and writes block. Use the two-phase pattern: `ADD CONSTRAINT chk_period_order CHECK (...) NOT VALID` (instant, holds lock for ~10ms) followed by `VALIDATE CONSTRAINT chk_period_order` (acquires only ShareUpdateExclusiveLock — concurrent with reads/writes).
Same blocking pattern as constraint 1 — ADD CONSTRAINT without NOT VALID on a 90M-row table is a multi-minute outage during peak billing traffic.

Same blocking issue — and worse: chk_status_values may FAIL outright if any existing row has a status value outside the enumerated set (legacy 'paused', 'pending', etc.). Pre-validation step required: `SELECT DISTINCT status FROM subscriptions WHERE status NOT IN (...)`. Use NOT VALID then audit before VALIDATE.

CREATE INDEX without CONCURRENTLY on a 90M-row table acquires SHARE lock blocking writes for the entire build duration. Although this is a partial index (only WHERE status = 'active'), index build still scans the whole table to identify matching rows. Use CREATE INDEX CONCURRENTLY.

CHECK constraint with status IN (...) is a poor model for an enumerated finite set. Consider a separate enum type (CREATE TYPE subscription_status AS ENUM ...) or a foreign key to a status reference table. Enums get planner statistics, the check IN list does not.
